assertion_results.text: Version impact table shows mixed results: 2.1.x versions marked YES (affected) and 2.2.x versions marked NO (not affected) based on lock file evidence
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md table: 2.1.0 = YES (h2 0.4.5 < 0.4.8), 2.1.1 = YES (h2 0.4.5 < 0.4.8), 2.2.0 = NO (h2 0.4.8 >= 0.4.8), 2.2.1 = NO (h2 0.4.8), 2.2.2 = NO (retag of 2.2.1), 2.2.3 = NO (h2 0.4.9), 2.2.4 = NO (h2 0.4.9). Stream-Level Summary confirms: '2.1.x: ALL versions affected', '2.2.x: NO versions affected'.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction includes only the actually affected versions (2.1.x versions), not the unaffected 2.2.x versions (Step 3.2)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md: 'Proposed (lock file evidence) | RHTPA 2.1.0, RHTPA 2.1.1'. Changes Required table explicitly removes RHTPA 2.2.0 ('Not affected -- ships h2 0.4.8') and adds RHTPA 2.1.1. Only 2.1.x versions remain in the proposed set.

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks are created only for the affected stream (2.1.x), not for 2.2.x which already ships the patched version (Step 8 Case A scoping)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md: Task 1 (Upstream Backport) targets release/0.3.z (2.1.x stream), Task 2 (Downstream Propagation) targets rhtpa-release.0.3.z (2.1.x stream). Section 'Why No Tasks for 2.2.x': 'The 2.2.x stream ships h2 0.4.8 or later at every version (2.2.0 through 2.2.4). Since 0.4.8 is the fix version for CVE-2026-33501, no remediation is needed for the 2.2.x stream.'

assertion_results.text: The analysis treats the issue as unscoped (no stream suffix) and checks ALL versions across all streams (Step 1 Stream scope resolution, unscoped path)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md: 'The issue summary... has **no stream suffix** in brackets. Per the skill's stream scope resolution logic, this issue is treated as **unscoped** -- it covers all configured version streams (2.1.x and 2.2.x).' version-impact.md table includes all 7 versions across both 2.1.x and 2.2.x streams.

assertion_results.text: Cross-stream impact notice is NOT generated because the issue is unscoped — it covers all streams by definition (Step 8 Case B applies only to scoped issues)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md: 'Per the skill definition, Case B (cross-stream impact) is skipped for unscoped issues -- they cover all streams by definition.' No cross-stream impact comment or Case B section appears in the remediation output. Only Case A tasks for the affected 2.1.x stream are created.
